stories.title: Travelers Championship Final Round — Hovland vs Scheffler at TPC River Highlands

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
FINAL ROUND. Travelers Championship.

Viktor Hovland -20 leads by 1 over Scottie Scheffler -19.

Hovland shot 64 yesterday to flip the leaderboard. Scheffler shot 60 in R2.

One of the best Sunday setups of the year.

#GolfFanrecap #Travelers

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Scheffler had a 2-shot lead entering R3. Hovland outplayed him — 64 to 67 — and flipped it.

Now No. 1 in the world starts Sunday 1 back with everything to prove.

$20M purse. The final group tees off this morning.

#GolfFanrecap #PGATour

article.headline: Travelers Championship Final Round — Hovland Leads by 1 over Scheffler

research.facts:
Travelers Championship Final Round
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Tournament: 2026 Travelers Championship at TPC River Highlands, Cromwell, Connecticut
- Dates: June 25-28, 2026 (Final Round Sunday June 28)
- Round 3 leaderboard: Viktor Hovland -20 (leads by 1), Scottie Scheffler -19
- Hovland shot 6-under 64 in Round 3 to flip 2-shot deficit into 1-shot lead
- Scheffler shot 67 in Round 3
- R3 18th hole: Hovland made 6-foot birdie; Scheffler ran 25-foot putt 8 feet past and missed the return
- Purse: $20 million (Signature Event, no-cut limited field)
- Winner's share: $3.6 million
- Scheffler in Round 2 shot 60 (11 birdies, 1 bogey) — historic round
- Scheffler seeking 2nd win of 2026 (1st was American Express)
- Scheffler has 8 top-5s and 1 win in 2026; 157 consecutive weeks at No. 1 in world rankings
- Bradley was defending champion but not in final pairing
**Key Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- Scheffler's world ranking: No. 1 (157 weeks straight)
- American Express was Scheffler's first 2026 win

stories.title: KPMG Women's PGA Final Round — Ryu Leads, Korda 4 Back in Historic Chase

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Haeran Ryu leads the KPMG Women's PGA at -11. Brooke Henderson is one back. Nelly Korda is four back at -7.

Korda needs a miracle round to become only the third woman ever to win 3 consecutive majors.

History is still on the table.

#GolfFanrecap #LPGA

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Only Zaharias in 1950 and Inbee Park in 2013 have won 3 consecutive LPGA majors.

Korda already won the Chevron and the US Women's Open this year.

She's 4 back with 18 holes left at Hazeltine.

#GolfFanrecap #NellyKorda #LPGA

article.headline: KPMG Women's PGA Final Round: Ryu Leads, Korda 4 Back in Historic Chase

research.facts:
KPMG Women's PGA Championship Final Round
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Tournament: 2026 KPMG Women's PGA Championship at Hazeltine National, Chaska, Minnesota
- Dates: June 25-28, 2026 (Final Round Sunday June 28)
- 54-hole leaderboard: Haeran Ryu -11 (leads by 1 over Brooke Henderson -10)
- Nelly Korda at -7 (four shots back)
- Ina Yoon at -9 (three shots back from leader)
- Dewi Weber and A Lim Kim at -8
- Ryu's Round 3: 3 birdies and eagle on front nine (5-under 31 front), 4-under 68 total
- Korda chasing 3rd consecutive LPGA major in 2026 (won Chevron + US Women's Open)
- Only Zaharias (1950) and Inbee Park (2013) have won 3 consecutive LPGA majors
- Purse: $13 million (record)
- Korda Hall of Fame: 25/27 points; major = 2 pts; winning KPMG = 27, then 1 more major = HOF eligible
- Course: Hazeltine National Golf Club, 6,800+ yards
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Wyndham Clark won 2026 US Open at Shinnecock Hills (wire-to-wire, -4, 1 shot over Sam Burns)
- Clark's 54-hole total of 7-under 203 was lowest 36-hole score ever recorded at Shinnecock Hills
- Clark rose from 34th to 8th in OWGR after US Open win
- Prize: $4.5 million

stories.title: The Open Championship Preview — Mickelson Out, Record Crowd at Royal Birkdale

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Phil Mickelson is out of The Open. Tiger Woods didn't enter.

Royal Birkdale. July 16-19. 300,000+ fans expected — an all-time record.

The 154th Open Championship has a big field and two big absences.

#GolfFanrecap #TheOpen #RoyalBirkdale

article.headline: The Open Championship Preview: Mickelson Out, Record Crowd Expected at Royal Birkdale

research.facts:
The Open Championship
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Tournament: 154th Open Championship at Royal Birkdale Golf Club, England
- Dates: July 16-19, 2026
- Phil Mickelson withdrew — new allegations regarding explicit photograph shown to wife of fellow professional
- Tiger Woods did not file entry by deadline
- R&A CEO Mark Darbon: expects attendance to exceed 300,000 (all-time record)
- Over 1 million ticket applications received
- Field: 156 players; 117 exemption competitors confirmed
- New format: "Last Chance Qualifier" — 12 players compete over 18 holes Monday of Open week for final spot
- Final Qualifying held at: Burnham & Berrow, Dundonald, Royal Cinque Ports, West Lancashire
**Key Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- Scheffler defending (won 2025 Open Championship)
- Royal Birkdale located in Southport, England

stories.title: LIV Golf Running on Loans — Four Events Left, Financial Future Unclear

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
LIV Golf is running on loans to finish its 2026 season.

Saudi Arabia's PIF confirmed backing through end of 2026 only. Louisiana paid $3.2M under contract — the event was cancelled anyway.

Four events left. The future is uncertain.

#GolfFanrecap #LIVGolf

article.headline: LIV Golf Running on Loans: Four Events Left, Financial Future Unclear

research.facts:
LIV Golf Financial Situation
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- LIV Golf operating on loans to finish 2026 season
- PIF (Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund) backing confirmed through end of 2026 only
- Louisiana event was postponed citing summer heat; Louisiana invested $7 million; paid $3.2 million under contract
- Four events remain: UK, New York, Indianapolis, $40M Team Championship in Michigan
- Jon Rahm won Hong Kong (by 3 shots) and Mexico City (by 6 shots) in early 2026
- Rahm completed 9 of 13 events at time of reporting
**Key Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- Total PIF investment: $5 billion+
- Only $200M of $600M needed for continued ops received
- New board members: Gene Davis + Jon Zinman

stories.title: John Deere Classic Preview — July 2-5 at TPC Deere Run

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
John Deere Classic is next week. July 2-5 at TPC Deere Run in Silvis, Illinois.

$8.8M purse over the Fourth of July weekend. JT Poston and Ben Griffin among those committed.

Young players chasing their first PGA Tour wins.

#GolfFanrecap #JohnDeereClassic #PGATour

article.headline: John Deere Classic Preview: $8.8M Purse, July 4th Weekend at TPC Deere Run

research.facts:
John Deere Classic
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Tournament: 2026 John Deere Classic at TPC Deere Run, Silvis, Illinois
- Dates: July 2-5, 2026 (July 4th holiday weekend)
- Purse: $8.8 million; winner's share: $1.584 million
